---
title: "Evidence-Based Engineering"
subtitle: "Applied statistics, environmental engineering, and reproducible analysis"
---

# Vince Pileggi, Ph.D., P.Eng.

## Evidence-Based Wastewater Engineering and Applied Statistics

I develop and share public, non-confidential resources at the intersection of wastewater engineering, applied statistics, technology assessment, and reproducible technical reporting.

This site focuses on engineering statistics, wastewater treatment performance, reliability-based assessment, teaching, publications, and reproducible workflows using tools such as Quarto, R, Python, and GitHub.

::: {.callout-note}
## Educational and Professional Resource

This website is intended for educational and professional development purposes. It presents generalized engineering methods, statistical techniques, and reproducible workflows. Any examples are illustrative and do not represent specific facilities, organizations, clients, suppliers, manufacturers, technology proponents, or regulatory assessments.
:::

## Current Focus

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-6}
### Wastewater Technology Assessment

Reliability-based approaches for evaluating wastewater treatment performance using long-term field data, engineering interpretation, and probability-based metrics.

[Technology framework](technology-framework.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
### Engineering Statistics

Practical statistical methods for environmental and civil engineering, including reliability analysis, uncertainty, visualization, and reproducible reporting.

[Explore methods](research.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
### Reports and Technical Notes

Selected public, non-confidential technical notes, research summaries, and reproducible examples related to wastewater and environmental engineering.

[View reports](reports.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
### Teaching and Mentoring

Resources for students, engineers, and early-career professionals interested in applying statistics and reproducible workflows to engineering problems.

[Teaching resources](services.qmd)
:::

:::

## Guiding Principle

> Engineering decisions are strongest when they are supported by transparent evidence, reproducible analysis, and clear communication of uncertainty.

## Site Roadmap

This site will gradually include:

- public research and publications;
- wastewater technology assessment methods;
- engineering statistics tutorials;
- reproducible Quarto, R, and Python workflows;
- public presentations and academic material;
- selected non-confidential technical notes.
